latest.title: Mushrooms drink water without roots
latest.script_key: watch these drink dewdrops cling to mushroom caps like tiny glasses fungi soak up water straight through their surfaces because unlike plants they don t have roots that s why you ll find them bright after rain even underground ever spotted one holding a drink like this

latest.title: Fungi weave hidden messages under the forest
latest.script_key: ferns aren t just green those lush fronds sit on a hidden network of mushroom threads that send signals like underground wi fi they warn neighbors about thirsty bugs before the leaves even know that s why a forest this quiet still hums with quiet chatter would you trust a silent forest to carry your secrets

latest.title: Beetles taste like wine and you already stepped on them
latest.script_key: this beetle tastes like wine that s why the tram kicks it off the tracks see its tiny feet those aren t just legs they re taste buds prague s street beetles survive by drinking leftover alcohol in spilled drinks that s why they show up near tram stops at night

latest.title: Mangroves take power out of waves
latest.script_key: mangroves can slow waves before they hit land watch the roots in the water because mangroves create a rough barrier that breaks up wave energy they also shelter young fish and hold muddy coastlines together restoration can be infrastructure made of roots which repair story next
